title: Mangrove Deforestation Detection And Coastal Protection Loss In Southeast Asia
summary: Build a verified workflow for detecting mangrove deforestation and estimating coastal protection service loss in Southeast Asia, linking satellite-derived mangrove maps, storm-surge models, and coastal-population data.
why_it_matters: Southeast Asia has lost over 30 percent of its mangroves since 1980, primarily to shrimp aquaculture and coastal development. Mangroves reduce storm-surge height by 30-70 percent and provide nursery habitat for fisheries. Global Mangrove Watch provides annual mangrove maps at 25m resolution, but linking deforestation to quantified coastal-protection loss has not been done systematically.

success_metrics.target: Deforestation detection validated against reference data with error statistics.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
